data.title: Tariff Uncertainty Means Investors Need Diverse Portfolios — Market Talk
data.description: Heightened uncertainty over the trade tariffs U.S. President Trump could implement means investors need to ensure they have diverse portfolios and employ hedging strategies, says UBS Global Wealth Management's Mark Haefele. "While the delay in implementation limits immediate market impact, the risk of retaliation remains," the chief investment officer says. Within equities, capital preservation strategies could be necessary to prevent losses if stocks turn lower. UBS GWM favors high-grade and investment-grade bonds as a way to diversify portfolios as these offer "insulation against uncertainty." (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

data.title: Chinese EV Maker Formed by Zeekr and Lynk & Co Sets Ambitious Sales Target
data.description:
By Jiahui Huang

Zeekr Group, the company formed through the acquisition of Lynk & Co by Geely's Zeekr Intelligent Technology, has set an ambitious goal to overtake foreign luxury carmakers in China this year.

The company said in a statement Friday that it aims to sell 710,000 vehicles in 2025, reaching a growth rate of 40%. Zeekr Group plans to "fully surpass BMW, Mercedes-Benz and Audi in the China market" and hopes to become a leading player in the luxury electric-vehicle sector within the next two years, it said.

China's electric-car market has experienced strong growth in recent years, with sales of battery EVs and plug-in hybrids surpassing that of conventional cars last year. As Chinese EV companies make new models faster, cheaper and with more advanced features, sales of vehicles produced by international joint ventures have come under pressure.

In 2025, Lynk & Co will release two new models, targeting annual sales of 390,000 units, while Zeekr aims to sell 320,000 vehicles by launching three new models launches, according to the statement.

Write to Jiahui Huang at jiahui.huang@wsj.com

data.title: Latour acquires heat pump and chiller manufacturer American Geothermal and increases the presence in the American market
data.description:
Investment AB Latour

Investment AB Latour has, through its wholly-owned subsidiary Swegon Group AB acquired American Geothermal, an American manufacturer of heating and cooling systems for the concrete industry as well as heat pumps and chiller products for healthcare, commercial, industrial, government and institutional buildings. Through the acquisition, Swegon strengthens its presence in the North American market.

American Geothermal has a strong position and legacy in manufacturing batchwater heating and cooling equipment for the concrete industry. This expertise has paved the way for the development of heat pumps and chillers tailored for the commercial and HVAC sectors, which are sold under the ChillMaster name. Founded in 1981, American Geothermal has 40 employees with headquarters in Murfreesboro, Tennessee. Sales amounted to 14 MUSD in 2024.

“As green environmental initiatives and regulations continue to evolve, we see great potential to grow our heat pump and chillers business in North America and complete our BlueBox offering. This acquisition marks a milestone in our strategy to increase our presence in North America. In addition, we are excited to add a US manufacturing site with great people and extensive experience and knowledge to our team”, says Andreas Örje Wellstam, CEO Swegon Group.

“There is a strong urgency to decarbonise HVAC systems in both new and existing buildings, and American Geothermal offers all electric solutions that use advanced technology to both heat and cool water. We are happy to become part of the Swegon North America family and look forward to continue expanding both our American Geothermal and ChillMaster businesses. Together with Swegon’s well-established sales network across the US and Canada we especially look forward to create new opportunities to expand ChillMaster’s product reach”, says Tom Anderson, CEO American Geothermal.

As an effect of the acquisition, the net debt (excl. IFRS 16) of the Latour Group increases to approximately SEK 12.7 billion compared to the net debt level at the end of December 2024, all else equal.

Göteborg, 14 February, 2025

INVESTMENT AB LATOUR (PUBL)

Johan Hjertonsson, CEO

For further information, please contact:

Andreas Örje Wellstam, CEO Swegon +46 31 89 58 00

Tove Nyqvist, Corporate Development, +46 31 89 58 00

Swegon Group is a market leading supplier in the field of indoor environment, offering solutions for ventilation, heating, cooling and climate optimisation, as well as connected services and expert technical support. Swegon has subsidiaries and distributors all over the world as well as 23 production plants in Europe, North America and India. The company employs 3,700 people and an annual turnover of 9.3 billion SEK.

Investment AB Latour is a mixed investment company consisting primarily of a wholly-owned industrial operations and an investment portfolio of listing holdings in which Latour is the principal owner or one of the principal owners. The investment portfolio consists of ten substantial holdings with a market value of SEK 94 billion as of 31 January, 2025. The wholly-owned industrial operations have an annual turnover of SEK 27 billion.

https://news.cision.com/investment-ab-latour/r/latour-acquires-heat-pump-and-chiller-manufacturer-american-geothermal-and-increases-the-presence-in%2Cc4105880

Press release 2025-02-14

data.title: Kadant to Replace Inari Medical in S&P SmallCap 600
data.description:
Kadant will replace Inari Medical in the S&P SmallCap 600 index on Feb. 19, S&P Dow Jones Indices said late Thursday.

S&P 500 company Stryker is acquiring Inari in a deal to be completed soon, the indexing and market research firm said.

data.title: Dutch Economy Expands 0.9% in 2024
data.description:
The Netherlands' economy expanded by 0.9% in 2024, up from a modest 0.1% growth in 2023, according to preliminary estimates.

Government consumption was the primary driver, rising by 3.1%.

Household consumption also increased, growing by 1.1%.

The trade balance made a positive contribution, with exports rising by 0.4%—driven by growth in services despite a decline in goods exports—while imports edged up by 0.1%.

Investment contributed slightly negatively to overall growth.

Among sectors, healthcare, business services, and trade played the most significant roles in boosting the economy in 2024.

data.title: Hermes CEO: The Evolution of Geopolical Relations Is a Concern
Chahua Modern Housewares Injects 45 Million Yuan Into Subsidiary to Drive Strategic Growth
data.description:
Chahua Modern Housewares approved a 45 million yuan capital increase for its wholly-owned subsidiary, Shenzhen Damai Technology Intelligent, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The move, approved by the board without requiring shareholder approval, aims to enhance operational capacity and accelerate the company's transition toward product-driven solutions.

Post-investment, Damai Intelligent's registered capital will rise from 5 million yuan to 50 million yuan.

data.title: South Korea's Jobless Rate Drops in January
data.description:
South Korea's unemployment rate dropped sharply in January, highlighting how domestic political tensions have had minimal impact on the country's jobs market.

The unemployment rate dropped to 2.9% from 3.7% in December 2024, according to Friday data from Statistics Korea.

On the other hand, employment for those aged between 15 years old and 64 years old rose 0.1 percentage points to 68.8%.

The number of employed people exceeded the number of unemployed. About 135,000 people were employed, with the number of employed rising to 27.9 million people. The number of jobless people rose only by 11,000 to around 1.1 million people in January, the Statistics Bureau reported.

Public administration and health and social recorded the most hires among all industries, adding 65,000 people and 180,000 people, respectively.

"We found today's figures positive, as they suggest that the political upheavals in December had a minimal impact on the labor market," ING's South Korea and Japan senior economist, Min Joo Kang, said. "At the same time, it shows that the Korean labor market is heavily dependent on public programs, rather than the private sector."

South Korea's political landscape was shaken, especially in the first part of the year, after former President Yoon Suk Yeol was impeached, following the announcement of an emergency martial law.

data.title: China Datang Renewable Power to Begin Payments for 1.3 Billion Yuan Debt on Feb. 21
data.description:
China Datang Corporation Renewable Power will start paying the principal and interest of its third tranche of the 2024 ultra-short-term debt worth 1.3 billion yuan on Feb. 21, a Thursday bourse filing said.

The company issued the debentures on Dec. 3, 2024, with a validity term of 79 days and a 1.87% interest rate.

data.title: Arm to Launch In-House Chip, the Financial Times Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
• Semiconductor company Arm Holdings plans to launch its own chip this year, in a departure from the SoftBank-owned group's business model, the Financial Times reports citing sources familiar with the company's plans.
  • Arm making its own chips is just one step in among larger plans to move into AI chip production, and the company will roll out its first in-house chip as early as this summer, the sources tell the FT.
  • The chip is expected to be a central processing unit for large data-centre servers, and the chips' base can be customised for clients including Meta. Production will be outsourced to a manufacturer like TSMC, the FT reports, citing the sources.
  • Arm, SoftBank and Meta declined to comment to the FT.

Full story: https://shorturl.at/W706r

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Shenzhen Soling Industrial Completes Setting Up of New Subsidiary
data.description:
Shenzhen Soling Industrial , through a unit Wuxi Soling Technology, completed the establishment of a 20 million yuan technical service subsidiary, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The Chinese car informatic devices manufacturer received the business registration procedures for the new subsidiary, Chengdu Soling Autolink Technology, the filing said.

data.title: India's Hexaware Tech IPO fully subscribed on final day of bidding
data.description:
** India's Hexaware Technologies 87.50 billion rupees ($1.01 bln) IPO fully subscribed on day 3 of bidding, exchange data shows

** Carlyle Group-backed IT firm's IPO receives bids worth over 90.31 billion rupees at the upper end of the 674-708 rupees price band

** Qualified institutional buyers bid for nearly five times the shares reserved - exchange data

** Non-institutional investors and retail subscriptions muted at hardly a tenth of shares on offer - exchange data

** HEXW, earlier this month, cut its IPO size to 87.5 billion rupees from 99.5 billion rupees

** No new shares will be issued in the IPO, while CA Magnum Holdings - affiliate of Carlyle Group - will sell part of its stake, prospectus shows

($1 = 86.8450 Indian rupees)

data.title: Malaysia's GDP Expands 5.1% in 2024
data.description:
Malaysia's gross domestic product rose 5% year over year in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to a Friday press release by the Department of Statistics Malaysia.

Economists polled by Reuters expected a 4.8% expansion.

Growth in Q4 was slower compared with the 5.4% growth experienced in the third quarter, the department said.

Malaysia's services sector saw GDP increase by 5.5% in Q4 compared with 5.2% in Q3, while manufacturing slowed to 4.4% from 5.6% in the previous quarter.

Overall, the economy grew 5.1% year over year in 2024, in line with the flash estimate, but faster than the 3.6% expansion in 2023.

data.title: Euro Could Rise Further on Tariff Relief But Gains Will Remain Limited — Market Talk
data.description: The euro could rise further on relief that President Trump's latest tariff plans aren't immediate, ING analyst Chris Turner says in a note. With tariffs to be assessed on a country-by-country basis in a report expected on April 1, trading partners have time to fine-tune strategies. However, the outcome could be "eye-wateringly large tariffs against some of the key countries with which the U.S. runs a goods deficit." The EU will "certainly be in the cross-hairs." That means the euro's gains should be limited to $1.0535-$1.0575 near-term, and it still risks falling to parity in the second quarter, he says. The euro rises to a two-and-a-half-week high of $1.0482, according to FactSet. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: Hermes shines after 'spotless' Q4
data.description:
** Shares in Hermes jump around 4% to a fresh record high after the Birkin bag maker posted better-than-expected Q4 sales

** Q4 sales came in at 3.96 billion euros ($4.14 billion), an 18% rise at constant exchange rates, ahead of analyst expectations for a 10% rise, according to a Visible Alpha consensus cited by UBS

** "Execution and delivery remain spotless," J.P.Morgan says, describing the results as "very solid"

** "Following Moncler last night, Hermes further confirms that during Q4, the luxury consumer was there, including in China, for the brands with solid momentum and execution," JPM adds

** The stock sits among the top performers of France's CAC 40 index and hits a new all-time high of 2,957 euros

** Peers LVMH and Gucci owner Kering rise 1.6% and 1%, respectively

data.title: Glenmark Pharma US Launches Latanoprost Ophthalmic Solution; Shares Down 5%
data.description:
Glenmark Pharmaceuticals Inc., USA, a unit of Glenmark Pharmaceuticals , has launched its Latanoprost Ophthalmic Solution, 0.005%, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The Latanoprost Ophthalmic Solution is bioequivalent and therapeutically equivalent to Upjohn's reference-listed drug Xalatan Ophthalmic Solution, 0.005%.

IQVIATM sales data estimates approximately $113.5 million in annual sales for the Xalatan Ophthalmic Solution in 2024.

Shares of the company were down over 5% in recent trade.

data.title: Huhtamaki rises on Q4 profit beat, strong Fiber Packaging unit
data.description:
** Shares in Huhtamaki rise almost 6% after the Finnish food packaging company posted its Q4 print with adj. EBIT that beat market expectations

** Its Q4 adjusted EBIT came in at EUR 110.3 million ($115.54 million), slightly above the EUR 104.7 million expected by analysts in Vara Research consensus

** "Q4 result narrowly exceeded comparables and our and consensus forecasts," Inderes analyst Antti Viljakainen says, "supported in particular by volume growth and efficiency measures included in the EUR 100 million savings programme"

** "On the quarterly scale, Fiber Packaging was the star of the show, turning in a cocktail of growth and savings to post excellent Q4 numbers," he adds

** If gains hold, the share is on track for best day since October 2024

($1 = 0.9547 euros)

data.title: Kazakhstan Jobless Rate Edges Down to 4.6% in Q4
data.description:
The unemployment rate in Kazakhstan edged down to 4.6% in Q4 2024, from 4.7% in the same period of the previous year.

The number of unemployed individuals increased by 2.4 thousand from a year earlier to 448.2 thousand, while the number of employed rose by 154.8 thousand to reach 9.20 million.

Additionally, the jobless rate for youth aged 15-34 ticked down to 3.3% from 3.4% a year earlier.

Meanwhile, the total labor force grew by 157.1 thousand to 9.65 million.

data.title: European shares set for eighth weekly gain; Hermes jumps after strong earnings
data.description:
European shares took a breather on Friday and were on track for their eighth straight week of gains, while luxury stocks jumped after robust earnings from Birkin bag maker Hermes.

The pan-European STOXX 600 index was down 0.1%, as of 0811 GMT. The index has closed at a record high for the past four sessions.

The benchmark was on track for its eighth straight weekly gain, its longest winning streak since March 2024.

Hermes rose 4.2% after the French luxury group reported an 18% rise in fourth-quarter sales, showing robust appetite from wealthy shoppers for expensive luxury items such as its Birkin bags.

Other luxury goods companies also rose on the day, with Burberry jumping 1.6%, Richemont up 1.4%, LVMH (LVHM.PA) adding 1.7% and Kering advancing 1.4%.

This gains lifted the European personal goods index (.SXQP) by 1.1%.

In contrast, heavyweight healthcare firms (.SXDP) lost 0.7%, dragged down by a 6% fall in Fresenius Medical Care after U.S.-listed Dialysis DaVita projected annual profit below estimates. DaVita's shares were down 11% in after-hours trading on Thursday.

Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump did not immediately impose reciprocal tariffs on Thursday, suggesting room for negotiations.

data.title: Tomra's shares at two-year highs after Q4 profit beat
data.description:
** Tomra shares rise more than 10% after the Norwegian recycling machinery maker beat Q4 profit expectations

** The group delivered Q4 adjusted EBITA of EUR 78 million ($82 million), beating a company-provided consensus by 13%

** On the revenue line, it posted a 5% beat at EUR 398 million

** It reported an increase in order backlog for both Recycling and Food business units, suggesting a positive shift in market sentiment, Jefferies says

** The stock is trading at its highest level in two years

($1 = 0.9544 euros)

data.title: Hermes CEO: All Countries Have Reported Growth in 2024 Including China
Infinite Alliance: Pioneering Global Innovation in Web3 and Blockchain
data.description:
IRVINE, Calif., Feb. 14, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Infinite Alliance, an innovative venture studio for Web3, made a significant impact at the WAGMI Conference, held from January 22-24, 2025, in Miami. As a pioneer in blockchain technology, AI, and DeFi, Infinite Alliance showcased its innovative solutions and strategic vision for the future of Web3.

The Infinite Alliance core team possesses unparalleled industry expertise and execution capabilities, having successfully launched three centralized exchanges into the global top 30 between 2015 and 2020. Our investment team consists of seasoned Wall Street bankers and venture capital experts with proven track records of leading tech companies to IPOs exceeding $10 billion in market capitalization. With a full-chain venture capital approach and a global resource network spanning North America, Asia, and Europe, we empower innovative projects with the capital, technology, and market access they need to succeed.

Infinite Alliance will provide early-stage blockchain projects with funding, resources, and strategic support to drive innovation and accelerate mass adoption. Specifically, this includes:

  • Organizing and participating in major blockchain hackathons, offering top-tier accelerator access for high-quality projects.
  • Facilitating potential investment opportunities from venture capital institutions.
  • Providing grant opportunities for innovative projects on leading blockchains.
  • Launch-as-a-Service (LaaS), offering one-stop deployment support for decentralized applications (dApps).
  • Delivering comprehensive ecosystem support, including infrastructure, security, fund management, business development, and marketing.

Driving Innovation in Web3

With a global reach and a deep understanding of the blockchain landscape, Infinite Alliance is positioned to lead the next wave of Web3 innovation. Our focus on strategic investments and early-stage incubation enables us to identify and support the most promising trends or projects in crypto.

"Infinite Alliance is at the forefront of exploring the intersection of AI, DePIN, and DeFi," stated J.C. Berry, a key member of the Infinite Alliance team, during his presentation at the recent WAGMI Conference. "By leveraging AI, Infinite Alliance is developing intelligent trading tools and risk management solutions." Berry further emphasized the transformative potential of DePIN, stating, "DePIN offers a promising avenue for tokenizing real-world assets and creating new economic models. This includes projects such as real-world assets, GPU, and supply chain logistics."

  • The growing importance of AI in DeFi: AI is transforming how we interact with decentralized finance, enabling more efficient and personalized trading experiences.
  • The rise of DePIN: DePIN has the potential to revolutionize industries such as real estate, supply chain management, and energy.
  • The need for interoperability: Cross-chain solutions are essential for building a truly decentralized and interconnected DeFi ecosystem.

Hopper, a next-generation multi-chain DeFi platform incubated by Infinite Alliance, exemplifies this innovative spirit. It offers a comprehensive suite of tools, including a powerful wallet, a high-performance DEX, a cutting-edge launchpad, seamless cross-chain bridges, and AI-powered market analysis. Hopper delivers intuitive trading features, ensuring a lightning-fast experience while making on-chain transactions accessible to everyone.

Infinite Alliance is committed to building a more decentralized and inclusive future. By investing in cutting-edge projects, incubating innovative startups, and fostering a vibrant community, we are driving the adoption of blockchain and Web3.

Contact:

Jing Lu

COO

UK's Wood Group's shares tumble on negative cash flow forecast
